basicInfo.tipsDescription: It is recommended to first figure out who this book is for, how it attracts readers, and what promises are made in the first 30 chapters, before adjusting writing mode, world boundary, and style choices. The settings here directly affect story planning, volume progression, and text generation.
basicInfo.sectionOrientationDescription: Define what kind of book this is, and whether it starts from scratch or continues from existing content.
basicInfo.leadSummaryPlaceholder: Use 2-4 sentences to describe the protagonist, core conflict, and key selling points.
basicInfo.novelLanguageHint: The language the AI uses when generating chapters, outlines, and character profiles.
basicInfo.genreDiffTipTitle: Difference Between Genre Base and Story Mode
basicInfo.genreDiffTipText: Genre Base answers 'what kind of book is this' (e.g., cultivation, urban, alternate history); Story Mode answers 'how does the story progress and deliver' (e.g., system-driven, invincible, slice-of-life).

basicInfo.estimatedChapterCountHint: Used as a reference for outline, beats, and pipelines. Can be adjusted later.

basicInfo.advancedSectionTitle: Narrative Experience & AI Collaboration Advanced Settings
basicInfo.advancedSectionDescription: These affect style and AI automation, but do not need immediate decisions.
basicInfo.worldSampleDescription: This is only for initialization reference. Please import, generate, or customize the actual world in the card above.
basicInfo.narrativeExperienceDescription: These define how readers perceive the work and affect the tone, density, and pacing of chapter generation.
basicInfo.aiCollaborationDescription: Defines how tasks are split between you and AI, and the level of automation.
basicInfo.resourceReadyScoreHint: 0 means just starting, 100 means settings, characters, and plans are fully ready.
basicInfo.postGenerationStyleReviewHint: When enabled, generated text is checked for AI artifacts and polished if issues are found.
basicInfo.continuationDescription: Continuation mode requires setting the upstream source, so it is expanded by default.
basicInfo.projectStatusDescription: Used for project management and pipeline triggers, not immediate decisions.
basicInfo.productionProgressDescription: Used for progress management. Can be adjusted per phase later.
